notes:
All ten ASI01-ASI10 confirmed. Titles verified against the authoritative OWASP GenAI Security Project announcement page (https://genai.owasp.org/2025/12/09/owasp-top-10-for-agentic-applications-the-benchmark-for-agentic-security-in-the-age-of-autonomous-ai/), which listed ASI01, ASI03, ASI04, ASI06, ASI07, ASI08, ASI09, ASI10 verbatim, and cross-validated against the repo's Appendix A ASI-to-LLM crosswalk (read directly from the Agentic Top 10 PDF) which is consistent across 2026/ entries. ASI10 = "Rogue Agents" (the item the task asked to supply) is triple-confirmed: repo crosswalk, the announcement page, and web search.

Title-variant caveats (recorded fuller Appendix A / repo-canonical forms):
- ASI02: recorded "Tool Misuse & Exploitation" (repo Appendix A form, matches task known-so-far). The announcement blog uses the shortened "Tool Misuse". Both denote the same risk.
- ASI05: recorded "Unexpected Code Execution (RCE)" (repo Appendix A form, matches task known-so-far). The announcement blog uses "Unexpected Code Execution" without the "(RCE)" suffix.
- ASI06: canonical form is "Memory & Context Poisoning" (confirmed by the announcement page); some 2026 repo prose shortens it to "Memory Poisoning" - not an alternate title, just abbreviation.

Secondary sources (DeepTeam, NeuralTrust, Auth0, Teleport) use minor rewordings for several titles (e.g., "Agent Identity & Privilege Abuse", "Agentic Supply Chain Compromise", "Cascading Agent Failures"); these were NOT used - the recorded titles follow the OWASP GenAI authoritative page and the repo's Appendix A crosswalk.

Source_url is the framework's official resource landing page; the resource page itself gates the risk list behind a PDF download, so titles were confirmed from the linked announcement page (URL above) rather than the landing page body. No element could not be confirmed. No IDs or titles invented.
